title: Plan a Stage 1 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 1 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 1 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 1 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Count” mean?
options: Say number names while touching each object
The question that asks for the total
The last number counted = the amount
A 2×5 grid that shows numbers to 10
questions.answer: Say number names while touching each object
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Count means Say number names while touching each object.

questions.question: What does “How many” mean?
options: The question that asks for the total
The last number counted = the amount
A 2×5 grid that shows numbers to 10
Words to compare two groups
questions.answer: The question that asks for the total
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: How many means The question that asks for the total.

questions.question: What does “3D shape” mean?
options: A solid shape you can hold; it takes up space
A box shape with 6 flat square faces
A perfectly round ball shape
A tin shape: flat round ends, curved side
questions.answer: A solid shape you can hold; it takes up space
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 3D shape means A solid shape you can hold; it takes up space.

questions.question: What does “Cube” mean?
options: A box shape with 6 flat square faces
A perfectly round ball shape
A tin shape: flat round ends, curved side
A flat part of a 3D shape you can touch
questions.answer: A box shape with 6 flat square faces
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Cube means A box shape with 6 flat square faces.

questions.question: What does “Whole” mean?
options: The complete thing before it is split
One of two equal parts of a whole
The same size as each other
Not the same size
questions.answer: The complete thing before it is split
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Whole means The complete thing before it is split.

questions.question: What does “Half” mean?
options: One of two equal parts of a whole
The same size as each other
Not the same size
A piece of the whole
questions.answer: One of two equal parts of a whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Half means One of two equal parts of a whole.

questions.question: What does “Tall / taller than” mean?
options: How far from the bottom to the top, standing up
Not far from bottom to top; used for height and length
How far from one end to the other, lying down
How far across, from side to side
questions.answer: How far from the bottom to the top, standing up
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Tall / taller than means How far from the bottom to the top, standing up.

questions.question: What does “Short / shorter than” mean?
options: Not far from bottom to top; used for height and length
How far from one end to the other, lying down
How far across, from side to side
Not far across; narrow
questions.answer: Not far from bottom to top; used for height and length
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Short / shorter than means Not far from bottom to top; used for height and length.

questions.question: What does “Add / plus” mean?
options: Put two groups together
How many when the groups are combined
Means the same as, or makes
Maths written with numbers and signs
questions.answer: Put two groups together
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Add / plus means Put two groups together.

questions.question: What does “Altogether / total” mean?
options: How many when the groups are combined
Means the same as, or makes
Maths written with numbers and signs
Remove some from a group
questions.answer: How many when the groups are combined
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Altogether / total means How many when the groups are combined.

questions.question: What does “on / under” mean?
options: On top of, or underneath, something
Higher than, or lower than (not touching)
At the back of, or at the front of
Right alongside, on either side
questions.answer: On top of, or underneath, something
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: on / under means On top of, or underneath, something.

questions.question: What does “above / below” mean?
options: Higher than, or lower than (not touching)
At the back of, or at the front of
Right alongside, on either side
In the middle of two things
questions.answer: Higher than, or lower than (not touching)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: above / below means Higher than, or lower than (not touching).

questions.question: What does “Sort” mean?
options: Put objects into groups by a rule
Things that belong together
How we decide what belongs
Words to compare two sets
questions.answer: Put objects into groups by a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sort means Put objects into groups by a rule.

questions.question: What does “Group / set” mean?
options: Things that belong together
How we decide what belongs
Words to compare two sets
Look at two sets side by side
questions.answer: Things that belong together
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Group / set means Things that belong together.

questions.question: What does “Week” mean?
options: A group of 7 days
Now, the next day, the day before
Which event or day comes first / next
The parts of a day
questions.answer: A group of 7 days
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Week means A group of 7 days.

questions.question: What does “Today / tomorrow / yesterday” mean?
options: Now, the next day, the day before
Which event or day comes first / next
The parts of a day
The thing that shows us the time
questions.answer: Now, the next day, the day before
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Today / tomorrow / yesterday means Now, the next day, the day before.

questions.question: What does “Teen numbers” mean?
options: The numbers 11 to 19
A group of ten ones
The single extra units
What a digit is worth by its place
questions.answer: The numbers 11 to 19
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Teen numbers means The numbers 11 to 19.

questions.question: What does “Ten” mean?
options: A group of ten ones
The single extra units
What a digit is worth by its place
Bigger than another number
questions.answer: A group of ten ones
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Ten means A group of ten ones.

questions.question: What does “3D shape” mean?
options: A solid shape that takes up space; you can hold it
A flat shape, like a shape drawn on paper
A flat surface on a 3D shape
The line where two faces meet
questions.answer: A solid shape that takes up space; you can hold it
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 3D shape means A solid shape that takes up space; you can hold it.

questions.question: What does “2D shape” mean?
options: A flat shape, like a shape drawn on paper
A flat surface on a 3D shape
The line where two faces meet
A rounded, not-flat surface
questions.answer: A flat shape, like a shape drawn on paper
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 2D shape means A flat shape, like a shape drawn on paper.

questions.question: What does “Whole” mean?
options: The complete thing before it is cut or shared
One of two equal parts of a whole
Exactly the same size or amount
Not the same size — so not halves
questions.answer: The complete thing before it is cut or shared
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Whole means The complete thing before it is cut or shared.

questions.question: What does “Half” mean?
options: One of two equal parts of a whole
Exactly the same size or amount
Not the same size — so not halves
A part of a whole
questions.answer: One of two equal parts of a whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Half means One of two equal parts of a whole.

questions.question: What does “Mass” mean?
options: How heavy something is
Has more mass / has less mass
A tool that tips down on the heavier side
Two things that weigh the same
questions.answer: How heavy something is
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Mass means How heavy something is.

questions.question: What does “Heavier / lighter” mean?
options: Has more mass / has less mass
A tool that tips down on the heavier side
Two things that weigh the same
How much a container can hold inside
questions.answer: Has more mass / has less mass
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Heavier / lighter means Has more mass / has less mass.

questions.question: What does “Add / plus” mean?
options: Put two amounts together to find the total
Start at a number and count forward
Remove some to find how many are left
Start at a number and count backward
questions.answer: Put two amounts together to find the total
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Add / plus means Put two amounts together to find the total.

questions.question: What does “Count on” mean?
options: Start at a number and count forward
Remove some to find how many are left
Start at a number and count backward
A row of numbers in order to help counting
questions.answer: Start at a number and count forward
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Count on means Start at a number and count forward.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information we collect about things
Put things into groups
The idea we use to sort
A set of things that are alike in one way
questions.answer: Information we collect about things
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information we collect about things.

questions.question: What does “Sort” mean?
options: Put things into groups
The idea we use to sort
A set of things that are alike in one way
A chart that uses pictures to show data
questions.answer: Put things into groups
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sort means Put things into groups.

questions.question: What does “Week” mean?
options: A group of seven days
The day before today
The day it is right now
The day after today
questions.answer: A group of seven days
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Week means A group of seven days.

questions.question: What does “Yesterday” mean?
options: The day before today
The day it is right now
The day after today
One of the twelve parts of a year
questions.answer: The day before today
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Yesterday means The day before today.
